name: Umm Kulthum
title: Lioness of Hashem
shortDescription: The daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin (AS) and sister of Imam Hussain (AS) who, alongside Lady Zaynab (SA), led the Karbala captives.
fullStory:
Hazrat Umm Kulthum (SA) is the daughter of Amir al-Mu'minin Ali (AS) and Hazrat Fatima Zahra (SA). She was the loyal sister of Imam Hasan (AS), Imam Hussain (AS), and Lady Zaynab (SA), who was present shoulder to shoulder with her brother and sister throughout all stages of the Ashura uprising.

Umm Kulthum was a very brave, eloquent, and learned woman. On the day of Ashura, she was one of the main pillars of support for the women and children in the tents and, alongside Lady Zaynab (SA), calmed the household after the companions were martyred. When the tents were set on fire, she selflessly rescued the children from the flames.

Umm Kulthum's key role began after the event of Ashura and during captivity. She delivered a crushing and historic sermon in Kufa that made the people of Kufa weep. In her sermon, she cried out: 'Woe unto you, O people of Kufa! Why did you abandon Hussain, kill him, and loot his belongings?'

Throughout the captivity route from Kufa to Sham, Umm Kulthum used her penetrating words to uncover the crimes of Yazid and Ibn Ziyad, preventing the Umayyad propaganda machine from distorting the truth of Ashura.

After returning to Medina, like Lady Zaynab (SA), she continued to enlighten people through elegies and weeping for the Master of Martyrs, and passed away some time after the event of Ashura.
quoteArabic: يا أَهْلَ الكُوفَةِ سَوْأَةً لَكُمْ، ما لَكُمْ خَذَلْتُم حُسَيْناً وَقَتَلْتُمُوهُ...
quote: (In the sermon of Kufa:) Woe unto you, O people of Kufa! Why did you abandon Hussain and kill him?
